text.title: Freelance Rate Jump Tactics Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules grounding Freelance Rate Jump Tactics. Each pairs a binary check + sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Target rate MUST cite the new niche's market median + p75 from explicit research sources (≥3 data points).
rule.rationale: Without market band citation, rate is arbitrary and hard to defend with prospects.

rule.statement: Existing clients MUST receive written notice ≥60 days before the new rate applies. Retroactive rate changes are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Short notice damages relationships and fairness; 60 days is industry-norm minimum.

rule.statement: The rate jump MUST be backed by ≥3 published case studies in the new niche with measurable outcomes (revenue lifted, hours saved, conversion gained).
rule.rationale: Without proof, prospects negotiate the rate back to the legacy band.

rule.statement: New rate applies immediately to new leads; existing clients on graduated timeline (60-90 days). NO same-day shift for existing clients.
rule.rationale: Same-day shift = mass churn; graduated rollout limits damage to 1-2 client losses.

rule.statement: Work-in-progress at the time of the notice MUST be billed at the OLD rate until natural completion. Retroactive billing is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Retroactive changes trigger lawsuits and reputation damage.
